_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner. Electrical work must be performed by a licensed electrician per AS/NZS 3000 and AS/NZS 3012; equipotential bonding around pools per AS/NZS 3000 Section 6.4. Lume's role is coordination + supervision; the licensed electrician is the responsible person for the work.

activity_name: Pool electrical bonding + light + equipment rough-in (licensed electrician)

hazards.title: Electric shock — energised wiring
hazards.risk: Contact with energised wiring during pre-commissioning testing

controls.title: All work performed dead — isolated, locked out, tagged out at switchboard
All work performed by licensed electrician per AS/NZS 3000
Test-before-touch with verified tester on every conductor before work

hazards.title: Fall into pool shell while installing
hazards.risk: Electrician working in / around excavation during conduit rough-in and light installation

controls.title: Edge protection or covers in place; only opened for direct work
Buddy on ground level during in-shell work

hazards.title: Cuts from conduit cutting / fishing
hazards.risk: Sharp conduit ends; fishing wire injury

hazards.title: Concrete contact (penetration packing)
hazards.risk: Wet concrete used to pack conduit penetrations

controls.title: Chemical-resistant gloves when handling wet patching mix

tasks: Pre-start: licensed electrician on site; confirm licence + recent CPR ticket; drawings on site.
Confirm isolation point at switchboard; lock-out tag-out applied.
Install bonding conductor around pool shell per AS/NZS 3000 6.4 (continuous loop, all reinforcement bonded).
Install conduit runs for pool light, pump, filter, heater, salt chlorinator, controller, garden lighting from approved switchboard location.
Install pool light niche / point per fitting manufacturer's instructions.
Pull cables; leave appropriate slack for connection.
Test continuity of bonding loop; record reading in Hub.
Test insulation resistance per AS/NZS 3000; record in Hub.
Electrician issues Electrical Safety Certificate of Compliance on completion; upload to Hub.
Tag-out remains in place until final commissioning.
